info.title: Incident management for federated SMC
info.description:
This is the incident management Swagger UI API specification for federated
SMC capability (last update: May 25, 2021).

externalDocs.description: Find more info about the federated SMC here

post.description:
Creates an incident in the SMC system (adapted from TMForum for
federated SMC)

204.x-custom-comment: In Service Interface Profile the return value is deviant set to HTTP 204 instead 200

post.description: Sets the communication endpoint address the service instance must use to deliver information about its health state, execution state, failures and metrics.

requestBody.description: Data containing the callback endpoint to deliver the information

delete.description: Resets the communication endpoint address the service instance must use to deliver information about its health state, execution state, failures and metrics.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the registered listener

date.x-custom-comment: changed from 'date' to 'date-time' due to service interface profile

TroubleTicket.x-custom-comment: This definition is not directly used (by reference). Instead, it alows the easy comparison for each property of 'TroubleTicket' between the original specification and the federated SMC extensions. To be consistent, each property used in this specification should be copied from this 'master' definition.

id.x-custom-comment: Restricted to federated SMC specific pattern

severity.x-custom-comment: Restricted to federated SMC specific pattern
severity.description:
TM Forum severity corresponds to ITIL incident impact.
- high - TM Forum severity corresponds to ITIL incident impact. Definition for high: Another MNE is affected and the overall impact is high.
- medium - Definition for medium: Another MNE is affected and the overall impact is medium or low.
- low - Definition for high: Another MNE is NOT affected and the local impact is high, medium or low.

creationDate.x-custom-comment: changed from 'date' to 'date-time' due to service interface profile

targetResolutionDate.x-custom-comment: changed from 'date' to 'date-time' due to service interface profile

status.x-custom-comment: Restricted to federated SMC specific status values. InProgressHeld and InProgressPending are moved to new property 'subStatus'
status.description:
The current status of the incident. If the status is "InProgress" the
subStatus field might be used to indicated a pending situation.
- Submitted - The initial state of an Incident when created by a TT
originator.
- Rejected - The Trouble Ticket was rejected because it:
  - is not submitted
  - provides invalid information
  - fails to meet the Business Rules in respect of the Product which originator is raising a Trouble Ticket against
  - is otherwise defective
- Acknowledged - The Incident was accepted and allocated a unique
Incident ID by the Incident handler.
- InProgress - The Incident was validated by the Incident handler and is
being processed.
- Resolved - The Fault indicated in the Incident was corrected by the
Incident handler and acknowledgement is awaited from its originator.
- Closed - The Incident originator has acknowledged the Resolved
state of the Incident, or the timeframe (default value 14 days) for
acknowledgement has passed without response from TT originator.
- Cancelled - The Incident which was sent from the originator to the
Incident handler was technically formatted correctly and was wherefore
acknowledged in first place, but the content on the Incident is
inadequate. Therefore, the Incident handler rejects to work on this
Incident.  Reasons for Incident cancellation are:
  - wrongly assigned
  - information provided is inadequate.

subStatus.x-custom-comment: At specification time of the SIP document it was not known, that the Open SPI specification file is the 'master' of all specification documents for TM Forum
subStatus.description:
The current sub status of the incident.
- Held - The Incident handler is awaiting further confirmation on
details of a Fault from originator before it can progress the Fault. An
example is where Appointment information is required.
- Pending - The Incident handler is confirming further details
internally before completing an Incident.  An example is where Incident
handler for network infrastructure spare parts to progress with the
Fault rectification.

resolutionDate.x-custom-comment: changed from 'date' to 'date-time' due to service interface profile

relatedParty.x-custom-comment: Restricted to federated SMC specific key value pairs
relatedParty.description: Extended objects for federated mission network operations.

relatedObject.x-custom-comment: Restricted to federated SMC specific key value pairs
relatedObject.description: Extended objects for federated mission network operations.

note.x-custom-comment: Restricted to federated SMC specific notes
note.description:
The Note object array contains numerous log entries of the incident. It
may contain first diagnosis, progress information and solution
description as note records.

TroubleTicket_Create.description: Attributes for creating a new incident (POST-method).

TroubleTicket_AppendOrUpdate.description: Attributes for updating or appending to a existing incident (PATCH-method).
TroubleTicket_AppendOrUpdate.x-custom-comment: Following 'oneOf' ensures, that only one of the epcefications fits

TroubleTicket_Append.description: Attributes for appending a note to an existing incident (PATCH-method).

TroubleTicket_Update.description: Attributes for updating a existing incident (PATCH-method).

TroubleTicket_Update1.description: For all states without 'Resolved' and 'Cancelled' without additional mandatory attributes

TroubleTicket_Update2.description: For the states 'Resolved' and 'Cancelled' with additional mandatory attributes

TroubleTicketResponse_Create.description: Attributes for response of creating a new incident (POST-method).

TroubleTicketResponse_Get.description: Attributes for response when requesting an incident (GET-method).

NoteFsmcExt.description:
The Note object array contains numerous log entries of the incident. It
may contain first diagnosis, progress information and solution
description as note records.
NoteFsmcExt.x-custom-comment: Following 'allOf' ensures, that a) the original specification must be valid and b) more restricted smc properties are valid

date.description: Timestamp, when the note was created

RelatedObjectFsmcExt.description: Extended attributes for federated mission network operations.
RelatedObjectFsmcExt.x-custom-comment: Following 'allOf' ensures, that a) the original specification must be valid and b) more restricted common smc properties are valid and c) more restricted key-specific smc property values are valid

id.description:
Contains a value. If href is filled, FSMID of the specific record
or object otherwise the value itself.

href.description: URI to specific record or object.

id.x-custom-comment: pattern ([A-Z]{3},)*([A-Z]{3}) does not longer fit because of i.e. 'EU EEAS only'

href.description: maxLength is prefix + max. base64 encoded data of size 4 MB (= 13 (prefix) + (4*1024 (KB) * 1024 (Byte) * 8 (bit) / 6 (bits/character)) + 2 (max padding)).

RelatedPartyFsmcExt.description: Extended objects for federated mission network operations.

Contains a value. If href is filled, FSMID of the specific record
or object otherwise the value itself.

href.description: URI to specific record or object.

code.description: Application related code (as defined in the API or from a common list)

reason.description: Text that explains the reason for error. This can be shown to a client user.

message.description: Text that provides more details and corrective actions related to the error. This can be shown to a client user.

referenceError.description: URL pointing to documentation describing the error
